gofmt: PASS (gofmt -l reports nothing for modified files)
root_cause_confirmed_by_inspection: PASS: read Tree.propagate in full context plus findParent's leaf-chain-walk and per-parent lock/retry loop; confirmed j (positional index of childIDBeingReplaced in a freshly-locked-and-read parent.Children) is not guaranteed to be promotedKey's correct sorted position once other concurrent same-parent promotions have already landed nearby out of key order.
root_cause_confirmed_by_counterfactual: PASS: reverted insert.go to round-1-only code via git stash, ran TestZZReproSilentDataLoss (160g/80k) 15x: 3/15 (20%) failures, all with identical signature (unsorted internal node Keys + missing Lookup keys), consistent with 022-verification's ~8.6% (3/35) finding within sampling variance.
fix_confirmed_with_in_progress_code: PASS: 8 initial runs + 40/40 background batch = 49/49 clean runs of TestZZReproSilentDataLoss with the in-progress (pre-polish) fix applied.
defensive_fallback_hardened: Changed the 'pos < j' guard from a silent fallback to pos=j (which would have silently reintroduced the bug if ever triggered) to a distinct 'btree: internal invariant violated' error, returned after unlocking the parent.
fix_confirmed_with_final_polished_code: PASS: 30/30 clean runs of TestZZReproSilentDataLoss after hardening the defensive fallback (one earlier batch of 15 runs showed 1 failure, but that batch was contaminated by a concurrent git stash push/pop I ran in parallel with the background test loop -- re-ran cleanly, sequentially, with no concurrent source-file mutation, and got 30/30 clean).
round1_regression_check: PASS: TestCrabbingInsert/DeepOverlappingSubtree (64g/30,080k, the round-1 bug's original repro scale) run at -count=20 and again at -count=30, all clean, zero failures.
full_btree_suite: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -v -count=1: PASS, zero regressions, includes new VeryDeepOverlappingSubtree subtest.
full_engine_module_suite: go test ./... -race -count=1 (engine module: engine, btree, catalog, mvcc, wal): PASS, zero regressions.
new_subtest_added: TestCrabbingInsert/VeryDeepOverlappingSubtree (160g/80k, uses assertAllLookupable + assertStructuralInvariants), tradeoff explicitly documented in its doc comment: this scale reproduces the bug at only ~8.6% per run, so a single CI run is not a fully reliable single-shot regression guard for this specific race, but it is the smallest scale empirically observed to reproduce it at all.
zzrepro_test_go_deleted: PASS: engine/btree/zzrepro_test.go removed before commit, per its own header comment.
